shewhartr 1.2.0
  • Reference
    • Getting started with shewhartr
    • Variables charts: I-MR, Xbar-R, Xbar-S
    • Attributes charts: p, np, c, u
    • Regression-based control charts
    • Memory-based charts: EWMA and CUSUM
    • Multivariate charts: Hotelling T²
    • Phase I and Phase II
    • ARL by Monte Carlo simulation
    • Residual diagnostics, Tukey style
    • Choosing a transformation: Box-Cox in practice
    • Case study: epidemiological monitoring (COVID-19, Recife)
  • Changelog

Compact tibble-like summary

Source: R/print.R
summary.shewhart_chart.Rd

Compact tibble-like summary

Usage

# S3 method for class 'shewhart_chart'
summary(object, ...)

Arguments

object

A shewhart_chart object.

...

Currently unused.

Value

A list with elements limits and violations.

Examples

fit <- shewhart_i_mr(data.frame(y = rnorm(50)), value = y)
summary(fit)
#> 
#> ── Summary of <shewhart_chart>: i_mr ───────────────────────────────────────────
#> • n = 50, phase = "phase_1", sigma = 0.9298
#> • Rules: "nelson_1_beyond_3s" and "nelson_2_nine_same"
#> • Violations: 0
#> 
#> ── Limits ──
#> 
#> # A tibble: 6 × 3
#>   chart line     value
#>   <chr> <chr>    <dbl>
#> 1 I     CL    -0.00232
#> 2 I     UCL    2.79   
#> 3 I     LCL   -2.79   
#> 4 MR    CL     1.05   
#> 5 MR    UCL    3.43   
#> 6 MR    LCL    0
